versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 1 This is the word that the LORD spoke through Jeremiah the prophet concerning Babylon and the land of the Chaldeans:
lift up a banner and proclaim it;
hold nothing back when you say,
her idols are broken in pieces.’
3 For a nation from the north will come against her;
it will make her land a desolation.
No one will live in it;
both man and beast will flee.”

context_lines: In **Jeremiah 50**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Jeremiah.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **A Prophecy against Babylon** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Jeremiah 50** remains the first control on meaning.
